nodes.label: ScheduleCronTool
nodes.summary: This project provides a **scheduling capability** for an AI agent, enabling it to create, list, and delete time-based tasks using standard *cron syntax*. It intelligently distinguishes between **session-only** tasks (in-memory) and **durable** tasks (persisted to disk), dynamically adjusting its behavior, prompts, and UI feedback based on feature flags and environment configuration.

children.label: Cron Tool Suite
children.summary: The core implementation of the three capabilities exposed to the AI agent: creating, deleting, and listing scheduled tasks. Each file defines a `ToolDef` object containing input/output schemas (Zod), validation logic, and the execution handler. Think of these as the specific "skills" installed into the agent's brain to handle time-based requests.

children.label: Dynamic Prompt Construction
children.summary: A system for generating the text instructions and descriptions fed to the AI. These are not static strings; they are built dynamically based on the current configuration (specifically, whether "durable" storage is enabled). This ensures the AI accurately explains to the user whether their scheduled task will survive a system restart or vanish when the session ends.

children.label: Durability & Persistence Logic
children.summary: The conceptual abstraction distinguishing between "session-only" tasks (in-memory) and "durable" tasks (persisted to disk). This logic is threaded through the schemas and prompts, controlling whether a task is written to `.claude/scheduled_tasks.json`. It's the difference between a temporary "timer" and a permanent "calendar event."

children.label: Tool UI Rendering
children.summary: A dedicated presentation layer using React components to display the results of tool interactions in the chat interface. It handles formatting how a "Scheduled Job" or "Cancelled Job" message looks to the user, separating the visual style from the backend logic. It ensures the user sees clean, human-readable summaries of complex scheduling actions.

children.label: Feature Gating & Configuration
children.summary: A centralized logic gate that determines if the scheduling system is active and what mode it runs in. It combines build-time flags, runtime GrowthBook feature flags, and environment variables to safely enable or disable functionality. It acts like a master circuit breaker that ensures the feature is only live when the environment supports it.
